title: AssertionReadResult
description: GET /assertions response (JM-ASSERT / ASSERTIONS_SIDECAR.md §7). Reads the resolved (LWW) assertions for an asset, queried by the portable asset_key (?asset_key= / ?content_hash=) or by inode/path resolved to asset_key (?inode= / ?path=). The Tier-B index answers; the sidecars are the durable truth behind it. assertions is ALWAYS a non-null array (empty when the asset has none). The assertion triple shape mirrors assertions-sidecar.schema.json#/$defs/assertion (inlined here so this schema validates standalone).

asset_key.description: The content-hash identity the assertions are bound to (`xxh3:<16 hex>` or a `path:<filename>` fallback). Empty when an inode/path query resolved to no assertions.

inode.description: Echo of the queried/accelerator inode when the query was by inode/path. Omitted for a pure asset_key query.

assertions.description: The resolved assertion triples, each verbatim JM-ASSERT shape (the accelerator inode is NOT projected — it is not part of a portable assertion).

key.description: Namespace-defined; e.g. "value", or a cluster_id for "person".

value.description: null is a retract (distinct from a force-negative value like log_profile "none").

asserted_at.description: RFC-3339 UTC; the LWW ordering field.
